programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | When Lightning Strikes
programme.desc: You've arrived at the only place flash floods, thunderstorms, avalances, and gale force winds will flash before your eyes one after the other. That's right, you're watching Extreme Weather Countdown, and we have an all-new lineup prepared today.

programme.title: Tales from the Edge
programme.desc: Ski to the North Pole. Row across the Pacific. Swim on Mt. Everest. Adventurers and brave souls who have pushed themselves to the limits of human endurance, and share what they've learned. 1) How one woman put man on the moon - Matt Porter and Margaret Hamilton 2) 3 things I learned while my plane crashed | Ric Elias 3) Why I study the most dangerous animal on earth -- mosquitoes | Fredros Okumu 4) How I swam the North Pole | Lewis Pugh 5) My 105 days in Taliban prison -- and a call to aid Afghanistan | Safi Rauf

programme.title: The Future Ahead
programme.desc: Get an awe-inspiring look at the future that awaits with visionaries already predicting, embracing and innovating on the potential of tomorrow. 1) Dear Facebook, this is how you're breaking democracy | Yaël Eisenstat 2) How to fight for democracy in the shadow of autocracy | Fatma Karume 3) What is deep tech? A look at how it could shape the future | Antoine Gourévitch 4) Welcome to the world of audio computers | Jason Rugolo

programme.title: Larger-Than-Life Art
programme.desc: Artists who translate imagination and poignant worldviews into monumental, magnificent pieces of art. 1) Public art that turns cities into playgrounds of the imagination | Helen Marriage 2) Why art thrives at Burning Man | Nora Atkinson 3) The chaotic brilliance of artist Jean-Michel Basquiat - Jordana Moore Saggese 4) Magical houses, made of bamboo | Elora Hardy 5) Walk with Little Amal, a theatrical journey celebrating the refugee experience | Amir Nizar Zuabi 6) The unexpected math behind Van Gogh's "Starry Night" - Natalya St. Clair

programme.title: Universe of Mystery
programme.desc: Unravel some of the most fundamental, yet unexplainable mysteries of the cosmos -- and find a few answers along the way. 1) How the James Webb Space Telescope will unfold the universe | John C. Mather 2) A rare galaxy that's challenging our understanding of the universe | Burçin Mutlu-Pakdil 3) Newton's three-body problem explained | Fabio Pacucci 4) Humanity's search for cosmic truth and poetic beauty | Maria Popova 5) The death of the universe | Renée Hlozek 6) Lessons from a solar storm chaser | Miho Janvier 7) After billions of years of monotony, the universe is waking up | David Deutsch

programme.title: The Business Angle
programme.desc: Insights from the professional world -- from CEOs, entrepreneurs, HR experts, everyday employees and more -- on how to run a successful business and make work life better for everyone. 1) How to break bad management habits before they reach the next generation of leaders | Elizabeth Lyle 2) Why being respectful to your coworkers is good for business | Christine Porath 3) Goats, blockchain and the future of money | Fariel Salahuddin 4) How to find the person who can help you get ahead at work | Carla Harris

programme.title: How The Brain Works
programme.desc: How does the brain -- a 3-pound snarl of nervous tissue -- create inspired inventions, the feeling of hunger, the experience of beauty, the sense of self? Researchers at the edge of science explain. 1) The brain may be able to repair itself -- with help | Jocelyne Bloch 2) What causes migraines? | Marianne Schwarz 3) Could we treat Alzheimer's with light and sound? | Li-Huei Tsai 4) How memories form and how we lose them | Catharine Young 5) What is so special about the human brain? | Suzana Herculano-Houzel 6) How the food you eat affects your brain | Mia Nacamull

programme.title: Good Talk
programme.desc: The art and science of meaningful conversation. 1) How to teach kids to talk about taboo topics | Liz Kleinrock 2) What baby boomers can learn from millennials at work -- and vice versa | Chip Conley 3) How we can protect truth in the age of misinformation | Sinan Aral 4) Empathy is not endorsement | Dylan Marron
